---
title: "Communication & Dissemination Track"
---

::: {.topic-badges}
Self-paced track · 10 topics · novice → advanced
:::

[← All self-paced tracks](../tracks.qmd){.track-back}

Use AI to write, translate, and disseminate public-health work — from grants and manuscripts to plain-language and multilingual materials. Every page assumes you've done **[Workshop 1](../workshop1.qmd)** (prompting and the Drexel data rules). The throughline: AI drafts; you verify and own every fact, citation, and claim.

## Difficulty levels

[Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} &nbsp; getting started — no prior experience needed.
[Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} &nbsp; comfortable with some AI use.
[Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} &nbsp; experienced users going deep.

## Topics

| # | Topic | Level | You'll be able to… |
|---|-------|-------|--------------------|
| 1 | [Science Communication & Plain-Language Translation](science-communication.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | Turn findings into briefs, fact sheets, and posts at the right reading level |
| 2 | [Grant & Proposal Development](grant-proposal-development.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Draft aims, biosketches, and justifications, then tighten to funder rules |
| 3 | [Literature Review & Evidence Synthesis](literature-review.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Build searches, screen abstracts, and summarize — verifying every citation |
| 4 | [Multilingual & Culturally-Adapted Materials](multilingual-materials.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Translate and culturally adapt outreach, with back-translation QA |
| 5 | [Manuscript Preparation & Peer Review](manuscript-peer-review.qmd) | [Advanced]{.level-badge .level-advanced} | Draft sections and reviewer responses; respect peer-review confidentiality |
| 6 | [AI for Design & Visual Communication](design-visual-communication.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Make graphics with Claude, Canva, Figma, and Adobe — matched to the data level |
| 7 | [Collaborating on Projects](collaborating-on-projects.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Set up shared Projects and custom GPTs for a team (companion to Workshop 3) |
| 8 | [Writing Your Course AI Policy](course-ai-policy.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | Write a clear, enforceable AI policy for your syllabus |
| 9 | [Assignment & Assessment Design in the AI Era](assignment-assessment-design.qmd) | [Moderate]{.level-badge .level-moderate} | Design assignments that develop competence whether students use AI or not |
| 10 | [Grant Administration with AI](grant-administration.qmd) | [Novice]{.level-badge .level-novice} | Track deadlines, format compliance docs, and draft progress reports |

## Not sure where to start?

Answer a few questions to find your starting point in this track.

<div class="readiness-quiz">
<script type="application/json" class="quiz-data">
{"questions":[{"q":"You use AI to draft a plain-language summary of a study for a community audience. What's the most important check before sharing it?","options":["Verify that every claim and statistic matches the original study","Make sure the summary sounds professional","Check that the AI cited its sources","Read it aloud to see if it flows well"],"answer":0,"explain":"Fact-checking AI writing is the core skill in this track. If you already do this routinely, start at topic 3 (Literature Review) or topic 5 (Manuscript Preparation). If not, start at topic 1 (Science Communication)."},{"q":"AI generates a literature review paragraph with three citations. What should you do?","options":["Look up each cited paper to confirm it exists and says what the AI claims","Trust the citations because they have proper formatting","Spot-check one citation and assume the rest are fine","Ask the AI if the citations are real"],"answer":0,"explain":"AI frequently fabricates citations. If verifying references is already your habit, skip ahead to topic 5 (Manuscript Preparation). Otherwise, topic 3 (Literature Review) will build this practice."},{"q":"You want to translate a health fact sheet into Spanish using AI. What's the best quality check?","options":["Have a fluent speaker review it and back-translate key sections","Use a second AI tool to check the first tool's translation","Confirm it reads well to you using a dictionary","Trust the translation if the AI model supports Spanish"],"answer":0,"explain":"If back-translation and cultural review are familiar, start at topic 4 (Multilingual Materials) or later. If this workflow is new, topic 1 (Science Communication) will build the foundation."}]}
</script>
</div>

::: {.callout-warning}
## Data rules apply throughout
Use **public or non-sensitive** data for every activity, and match any tool to Drexel's data classification. See [Tools & Access](../tools.qmd) and Drexel's [AI Tools page](https://drexel.edu/provost/ai/tools).
:::
